stats_fallback.lengthening_details: 24 m midsection at Fincantieri Palermo, 2 May - 4 July 2015; +194 passenger cabins, +59 crew cabins; Doremy Spray Park added; length 251 m → 275 m, beam → 32 m, GT ~60,000 → 65,591

related.class_notes: Lirica Class. All four ships received 24m Renaissance Programme lengthening at Fincantieri Palermo between 2014-2015. Each uses unique Italian MDR names: Opera has La Caravella + L'Approdo.

deployment.winter_2025_26: South Africa from Durban (2nd season after replacing MSC Musica in October 2024)
deployment.summer_2026: Eastern Mediterranean from Venice (Marghera) and Bari
deployment.winter_2026_27_onwards: Year-round La Romana, Dominican Republic (first sailing 16 November 2026). From April 2027 adds Fort-de-France, Martinique as second embarkation port. MSC's first year-round La Romana commitment.
deployment.south_africa_handover: MSC Armonia replaces Opera in South Africa starting with 1 November 2026 repositioning cruise from Marghera to Durban.

history.renaissance_lengthening: 2 May - 4 July 2015 at Fincantieri Palermo (first block laid 28 January 2015). 24m midsection inserted, 251m → 275m length, beam widened to 32m, +194 pax cabins, +59 crew cabins, GT ~60,000 → 65,591. Part of ~USD 273M Renaissance Programme covering all four Lirica-class ships.
history.venice_2019_incident: 2 June 2019: Collided with San Basilio Pier in Venice's Giudecca Canal, damaging Uniworld's River Countess. Cause: main electrical switchboard warning ignored by chief engineer and chief electrician. Captain Carmine Siviero received 5-month prison sentence (commuted to fine); shorter sentences for chief engineer, chief electrician, and two other crew. Incident accelerated Venice's ban on large cruise ships in the Giudecca.
history.south_africa: October 2024 replacement for MSC Musica in South Africa; 2025-26 second season
